title: Aureline operational runbook packet and action-ledger entry
description: Boundary schema for the runbook_packet_record and action_ledger_entry_record frozen in docs/ops/incident_workspace_contract.md. One record family freezes the operational runbook a responder reads when an incident-workspace points at it: the typed step grammar (diagnostic inspection, evidence capture, communication, mitigation that mutates target state, and approval-gate checkpoints), the sandbox-and-approval posture every mutating step inherits from the same execution-context / workspace-trust / approval-ticket vocabulary that ordinary runtime actions use, the preview-hash pin every mutating step computes before apply, and the outcome vocabulary every action-ledger entry records after the step ran. action_ledger_entry_record is append-only: prior entries never mutate and are never backdated; corrections land as new entries that supersede earlier ones by stable id. The record never carries raw provider URLs, raw provider payloads, raw stack frames, raw stdout / stderr, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw response bodies, raw alert payloads, raw operator identity strings, raw approval-ticket bodies, or raw secret material; rows carry opaque ids, typed vocabulary, redaction-aware reviewable labels (<= 1024 graphemes), and freshness / approval / target-context / outcome tokens only. Composes with (and does not replace) ADR-0007 (secret-broker handle / raw-secret-forbidden boundary), ADR-0009 (execution-context and scope), ADR-0010 (browser-handoff and approval-ticket envelope), ADR-0018 (workspace-trust state), schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json (target identity / sandbox posture / policy epoch), schemas/integration/approval_ticket.schema.json (approval-ticket envelope), schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json (browser-handoff packet envelope), schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json (emergency-action / revocation / break-glass linkage), and schemas/ops/evidence_handoff_bundle.schema.json (immutable-snapshot bundle the action-ledger feeds when a postmortem / review bundle is exported). The eventual ops crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every incident-workspace render, support / export reader, postmortem / review reader, AI-tool review surface, and admin / policy review reads. Adding a new record_kind, step_intent_class, sandbox_admission_class, approval_admission_class, action_admission_class, outcome_class, raw_ref_class, denial_reason, or audit_event_id is additive-minor and bumps runbook_packet_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

runbook_packet_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the runbook-packet vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, journal entries, support bundles, postmortem / review bundles, and admin reconciliation surfaces. Raw provider URLs, raw provider payloads, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw response bodies, raw alert payloads, raw operator identity strings, raw approval-ticket bodies, and raw secret material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary safe for incident-workspace render, postmortem / review, support / export, and admin reconciliation surfaces; never carries the raw payload.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in preview, postmortem / review, and export review surfaces. Never carries the raw payload.

preview_hash.description: Opaque content hash of the mutation preview computed before apply. The same value is recorded on the matching action_ledger_entry_record so a reviewer can confirm the applied mutation matches the previewed mutation. Raw payload bytes never appear; only the opaque hash crosses this boundary.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class re-exported from ADR-0010 / ADR-0007. Raw provider payloads, raw stack frames, raw stdout / stderr, raw absolute paths, raw command lines, raw response bodies, raw alert payloads, raw operator identity strings, and raw approval-ticket bodies never cross this boundary regardless of class. private_triage_only is admissible only when the runbook-packet is bound to a security-side incident_workspace_packet record.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0018 / ADR-0009 workspace-trust state the runbook-packet was admitted under.

policy_context.description: Policy-context envelope re-exported from ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 / ADR-0018 so downstream lanes can reason about the packet's policy provenance without re-deriving it.

target_context_class.description: Frozen target-context vocabulary. Names which execution context the runbook step or action-ledger entry resolves against. browser_handoff_external_target / console_handoff_external_target reflect attributable transitions out of the local control plane; target_unverifiable_user_review_required is the safe-default that strips admissible_under_* on every mutating step.

step_intent_class.description: Frozen step-intent vocabulary. Read-only intents (diagnostic_inspection_read_only, evidence_capture_read_only, communication_read_only, approval_gate_no_mutation) MUST resolve sandbox_admission_class to inspect_only_no_mutation and approval_admission_class to no_approval_required_read_only. Mutating intents inherit the same sandbox / approval posture as ordinary runtime actions. step_intent_unknown_requires_review is the safe-default that strips every admissible_under_* posture.

sandbox_admission_class.description: Frozen sandbox-admission vocabulary. Mirrors the ordinary runtime-action vocabulary so a mutating runbook step never carries a private 'incident-only' bypass. external_handoff_only_browser_or_console is admissible only on mitigation_mutating_browser_handoff / mitigation_mutating_console_handoff.

approval_admission_class.description: Frozen approval-admission vocabulary. approval_admitted_under_break_glass_event is admissible only when a non-null break_glass_event_ref is cited and the matching break-glass profile is admitted in artifacts/governance/signing_quorum.yaml. approval_admission_unknown_requires_review is the safe-default that strips admissible_under_* sandbox postures.

raw_ref_class.description: Frozen raw-ref-class vocabulary. Names which raw artefact the action-ledger entry links to by-reference. Raw bodies never cross this boundary; only the opaque ref is recorded. raw_ref_unverifiable_user_review_required strips admissible_under_* approval postures.

outcome_class.description: Frozen outcome vocabulary recorded on action-ledger entries after the step ran. captured_offline_pending_drain / external_handoff_pending_provider_callback describe attributable transitions: the entry stays open until the matching publish-later or callback envelope drains. outcome_unverifiable_user_review_required is the safe-default when the responder cannot classify.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event vocabulary for runbook-packet and action-ledger surfaces. Audit denials cite the matching denial_reason.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary. Apply paths cite a typed reason rather than describing the cause in prose. Closed list; new values are additive-minor.

ordinal.description: Stable ordinal of the step within the runbook. Steps are ordered by ordinal; gaps are admitted (a runbook may renumber on edit) and the matching action-ledger entries cite the step_id, not the ordinal.

preview_hash.description: Opaque preview-hash for mutating steps. Non-null when step_intent_class resolves to mitigation_mutating_under_runtime_approval / mitigation_mutating_under_break_glass / mitigation_mutating_browser_handoff / mitigation_mutating_console_handoff / automation_invoke_recorded_recipe; null on read-only intents.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket envelope (ADR-0010). Non-null when approval_admission_class is approval_admitted_under_runtime_approval_ticket; null otherwise.

break_glass_event_ref.description: Opaque ref to schemas/security/break_glass_event.schema.json. Non-null when approval_admission_class is approval_admitted_under_break_glass_event; null otherwise.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Non-null when step_intent_class is mitigation_mutating_browser_handoff; null otherwise.

console_handoff_session_ref.description: Opaque ref to the console-handoff session envelope. Non-null when step_intent_class is mitigation_mutating_console_handoff; null otherwise.

automation_recipe_ref.description: Opaque ref to schemas/automation/recipe_manifest.schema.json. Non-null when step_intent_class is automation_invoke_recorded_recipe; null otherwise.

allOf.description: Read-only intents MUST resolve sandbox_admission_class to inspect_only_no_mutation and approval_admission_class to no_approval_required_read_only.
Mutating intents MUST cite a non-null preview_hash.
approval_admitted_under_break_glass_event MUST cite a non-null break_glass_event_ref.
mitigation_mutating_browser_handoff MUST cite a non-null browser_handoff_packet_ref and target_context_class browser_handoff_external_target.
mitigation_mutating_console_handoff MUST cite a non-null console_handoff_session_ref and target_context_class console_handoff_external_target.
automation_invoke_recorded_recipe MUST cite a non-null automation_recipe_ref.
external_handoff_only_browser_or_console sandbox is admissible only on browser / console handoff intents.

step_record.description: One runbook step record. Composes the typed step grammar and the sandbox / approval / preview / target-context posture every responder reads before apply.

applicable_severity_class_set.description: Operational-severity classes the runbook is admissible against. Mirrors the operational-severity vocabulary in docs/ops/incident_workspace_contract.md and is distinct from the security-severity vocabulary in schemas/security/advisory_record.schema.json.

applicable_deployment_profile_class_set.description: Deployment-profile classes the runbook is admissible against.

step_records.description: Step records ordered by ordinal. The first entry SHOULD be a diagnostic_inspection_read_only or evidence_capture_read_only step so a responder reads the runbook in inspect-only posture by default.

runbook_packet_record.description: One runbook packet. Carries the typed step grammar, the sandbox / approval / preview / target-context posture every step inherits, and the policy / redaction envelope. Mutating steps inherit the same sandbox-and-approval rules as ordinary runtime actions.

incident_workspace_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the incident_workspace_record this entry was recorded against. The action-ledger is append-only inside the incident workspace.

runbook_packet_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the runbook_packet_record the executed step belongs to.

step_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the step_record.step_id. Stable across runbook renumber operations.

actor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the executing actor (responder pseudonym, on-call handle, automation actor). Raw operator identity strings never appear here.

preview_hash.description: Preview-hash recorded at apply time. Non-null when the step is mutating; equal to applied_preview_hash on a clean apply, distinct on a rolled-back or denied apply.

applied_preview_hash.description: Preview-hash observed on apply. Non-null when outcome_class is success_observed / success_under_partial_evidence / rolled_back_after_partial_apply.

publish_later_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to schemas/providers/publish_later_record.schema.json. Non-null when outcome_class is captured_offline_pending_drain; null otherwise.

provider_callback_envelope_ref.description: Opaque ref to schemas/providers/provider_callback_envelope.schema.json. Non-null when outcome_class is external_handoff_pending_provider_callback; null otherwise.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Opaque ref to the rollback-checkpoint record. Non-null when outcome_class is rolled_back_after_partial_apply; null otherwise.

raw_ref_id.description: Opaque ref to the raw artefact named by raw_ref_class. Null when raw_ref_class is no_raw_ref_required.

supersedes_action_ledger_entry_ref.description: Opaque ref to a prior action_ledger_entry_record that this entry supersedes. Non-null only when this entry is a correction landed after a typed denial or a partial apply; the prior entry remains visible in the chronology and is never deleted or backdated.

allOf.description: captured_offline_pending_drain outcome MUST cite a non-null publish_later_queue_item_ref.
external_handoff_pending_provider_callback outcome MUST cite a non-null provider_callback_envelope_ref.
rolled_back_after_partial_apply outcome MUST cite a non-null rollback_checkpoint_ref.
Read-only step intents MUST resolve preview_hash and applied_preview_hash to null and outcome_class to one of the read-only-admissible outcomes.
Mutating step intents MUST cite a non-null preview_hash.
approval_admitted_under_runtime_approval_ticket entries MUST cite a non-null approval_ticket_ref.
approval_admitted_under_break_glass_event entries MUST cite a non-null break_glass_event_ref.
raw_ref_class outside no_raw_ref_required and raw_ref_unverifiable_user_review_required MUST cite a non-null raw_ref_id.

action_ledger_entry_record.description: One append-only action-ledger entry. Recorded after a runbook step runs (or is denied / rolled back / captured offline / handed off externally). Prior entries never mutate and are never backdated; corrections land as new entries that supersede earlier ones via supersedes_action_ledger_entry_ref.
